content: Stroke risk stratification in non-valvular AF uses CHA2DS2-VASc score: Congestive HF (1), Hypertension (1), Age ≥75 (2), Diabetes (1), Stroke/TIA/thromboembolism (2), Vascular disease (1), Age 65-74 (1), Sex category female (1). Score ≥2 in men or ≥3 in women: oral anticoagulation recommended. Score 1 in men or 2 in women: consider anticoagulation based on patient values and preferences. Score 0 in men or 1 in women: no anticoagulation. DOACs (apixaban, rivaroxaban, dabigatran, edoxaban) preferred over warfarin except in mechanical valves or moderate-severe mitral stenosis (use warfarin). ARISTOTLE (apixaban), ROCKET-AF (rivaroxaban), RE-LY (dabigatran), ENGAGE-AF (edoxaban) trials showed DOACs noninferior or superior to warfarin for stroke prevention with lower intracranial hemorrhage risk. Bleeding risk assessed with HAS-BLED score (not to withhold anticoagulation but to address modifiable factors). Left atrial appendage occlusion (Watchman device) alternative for patients with contraindications to long-term anticoagulation.
